mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending power, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.3pp, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below random chance over a 24-month backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous monthly increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized strength in consumer demand and residential investment, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint positive MoM signal yields negative 60-day forward returns in > 60% of occurrences, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in the index of industrial production signals weakening manufacturing output and supply-side contraction, which triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days during late-cycle expansions.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines fail to predict negative 5-day returns during late-cycle periods, or the signal directional accuracy falls below 45% in a regime-filtered walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: A falling federal funds rate combined with stable or declining inflation indicates real rate compression and accommodative monetary policy, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS declines while CPIAUCSL MoM change <= 0.2
failure_condition: The combined signal produces negative 20-day returns in > 58% of triggered months, or the interaction effect vanishes when CPIAUCSL exceeds 0.3% MoM.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly declines in total nonfarm payrolls reflect weakening economic activity and reduced corporate revenue expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS declines show no statistically significant negative correlation with 60-day forward returns over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC remains below -0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth exceeding 3.0% annualized confirms robust aggregate demand and corporate earnings momentum, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days following data release.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative in > 55% of quarters where GDPC1 growth exceeds 3.0%, or the post-release return pattern shows no significant deviation from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between declining industrial production and rising unemployment signals a broad supply-demand mismatch and recessionary pressure, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days during tightening cycles.
failure_condition: The divergence signal fails to predict negative 20-day returns during tightening periods, or the predictive accuracy drops below 40% in a regime-specific out-of-sample test.
